raw_consumer_statement: XX/XX/XXXX, a message was sent to my phone indicating a charge on my Citi Card account that I have had for years. It was a charge to XXXX XXXX in another state. I reported this to Citi Card and canceled the card. The same day, Citi sent a replacement card. I received the card XX/XX/XXXX and immediately attempted to activate it only to find that it had already been activated. I called Citi to report that found out that the card was already activated and they indicated XXXX  had been charged to it on XXXX XXXX. I then received a statement indicating that these charges were made on XX/XX/XXXX, before I received the card in the mail. I have been battling with Citi ever since to clear up this information and restore my credit. I never activated the card myself. I have never used the card. The new one was canceled on the XXXX as well. I am still receiving bills for {$680.00} and more recently with interest applied increasing it to {$730.00}. I called Citi XX/XX/scrub> 2025 and again complained that I do not know the person and I did not spend XXXX  on XXXX on XXXX. I do not have an XXXX account and no need for a new phone. They keep saying this is under review when I call but are still billing and adding interest. Citi card login requires full card number which is unsecure. I believe this makes hacking easy. I could not login to my old account as I believe that was hacked as well.
